paper.title: Distinct patterns of hypogonadism in a cross‐sectional study of Malaysian men with type 2 diabetes mellitus: The role of insulin resistance, visceral adiposity, and aging

entities.name: Distinct patterns of hypogonadism in a cross‐sectional study of Malaysian men with type 2 diabetes mellitus: The role of insulin resistance, visceral adiposity, and aging

evidence_entities.text: Hypogonadism is common among male patients living with type 2 diabetes mellitus and can be divided into hypergonadotropic hypogonadism (hyper‐H) and hypogonadotropic hypogonadism (hypo‐H). Hypo‐H is strongly linked to insulin resistance and visceral adiposity, while hyper‐H in type 2 diabetes mellitus males has not been extensively studied.
Age is significantly associated with hyper‐H, while insulin resistance and visceral adiposity are strongly linked to hypo‐H.

hyper‐H and hypo‐H are two distinct conditions with different underlying pathophysiologies. Hypo‐H is often seen in patients with diabesity or metabolic syndrome, and this entity is known as male obesity secondary hypogonadism.

relationships.subject: hypogonadotropic_hypogonadism
relationships.note: Hypo-H in patients with diabesity or metabolic syndrome is known as male obesity secondary hypogonadism
